channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Remains Effectively Blocked After Naval Skirmishes
item.description: The U.S. Navy’s blockade continued to intercept ships trying to leave or enter Iranian ports, while fear of Iranian attacks kept other ships from attempting passage through the narrows.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Devon Lum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Tankers anchored in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Qeshm Island, Iran, last month.

item.title: Deadly Israeli Strikes Erode Cease-Fire in Lebanon
item.description: Fighting between Israel and Hezbollah has shown little sign of abating, with Hezbollah also continuing to attack Israeli troops, and both sides accusing the other of violating a U.S.-brokered truce.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Rescue workers searching for survivors under the rubble of houses damaged by an Israeli airstrike Saturday, in the village of Saksakiyeh in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
Long Overlooked, Caspian Sea Provides Strategic Trade Route for Iran
item.description: The landlocked body of water has taken on new significance, with Russia shipping military and commercial goods to bolster Tehran’s ability to withstand the U.S. assault.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish, Neil MacFarquhar and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A woman walks along the southern coast of the Caspian Sea in the port city of Bandar Anzali, Iran. The Caspian has emerged as a key trade route between Russia and Iran.

item.title: Iran Says It Seized Oil Tanker Listed by U.S. as Sanctions Violator
item.description: A maritime tracking company said the move was likely performative, given the Chinese-owned ship has an “established history within the Iranian trade ecosystem.”
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships in the Strait of Hormuz near Oman on Friday.

item.title: Emirates Doubles Down on U.S. and Israeli Ties Amid Iran War
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has borne the brunt of Iranian attacks throughout the war, hardening the government’s resolve to reassess its list of friends and foes.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Emirates leader, Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed, and President Trump in Abu Dhabi, last year.

item.title: Artists’ Strike Closes Pavilions at Venice Biennale, Adding to Upheaval
item.description: The hottest exhibitions at the world’s major art exhibition were shuttered on Friday as part of a pro-Palestinian demonstration.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall
item.media:credit: Casey Kelbaugh for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Netherlands’ shuttered Venice Biennale pavilion displayed a flag and fliers in support of Palestinians on Friday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi, Erica L. Green, Motoko Rich and Euan Ward

item.title: Trump Says Cease-Fire Is Intact After Trade of Attacks in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The president dismissed Iran’s firing on U.S. vessels in the strait as a mere “trifle.”
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump at the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., on Thursday.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Trade Strikes as Tensions in Strait of Hormuz Rise
item.description: The escalating attacks raise the threat that the cease-fire could break down.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: This photo released by U.S. Central Command Public Affairs shows the aircraft carrier U.S.S. George H.W. Bush on Sunday.

item.title: As Stockpiles Fall, U.S. Sells More Missiles Worth $17 Billion to Gulf Nations
item.description: The United States and its partners have burned through an enormous amount of air defense missiles in the Iran war, and production is slow.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A Patriot missile launcher in Kuwait City in 2019. The United States is selling Kuwait another $9.3 billion worth of interceptor missiles.

item.title: Iran and U.S. Consider One-Page Plan to End Hostilities, Iranian Officials Say
item.description: The plan would reopen the Strait of Hormuz and give the two countries 30 days to hammer out a comprehensive deal, according to three Iranian officials.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday near Fujairah, United Arab Emirates.

item.title: Israel Says It Killed a Hezbollah Chief Near Beirut, Testing the Truce
item.description: The strike was the first near the Lebanese capital since a cease-fire that has curbed fighting but not halted it. Washington is pushing for a lasting peace, hoping it will ease diplomacy with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Rescue workers on Thursday at a building in Beirut’s southern suburbs that was destroyed a day earlier by an Israeli strike.

item.title: House Republican Proposes Bill to Wind Down the Iran War
item.description: Representative Tom Barrett, a Michigan Republican facing a tough re-election race, introduced a bill to impose limits on the use of military force in Iran and end the fighting this summer.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Alfield Reeves for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Tom Barrett at a rally in 2024.

item.title: Shell Reports Nearly $7 Billion Profit After Oil Prices Surged Amid U.S.-Iran War
item.description: The oil giant’s earnings in the first three months of the year were more than double the previous quarter’s and follow similarly strong results of European rivals.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Schmidt and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Shell reported huge profits for the oil company in the first three months of the year.

item.title: U.S. Awaits Iran’s Response to Latest Peace Proposal to End War
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi, Pranav Baskar, Helene Cooper and Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Iran Says It Is Reviewing a U.S. Proposal to End the War
item.description: Nothing was disclosed about the peace plan, and there were conflicting signals from the Iranians about how seriously they were taking it.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands, Qasim Nauman, Mark Landler and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump at a Mother’s Day event at the White House on Wednesday. He said the Iranians “want to make a deal.”

item.title: How the Fight Over Israel Is Playing Out Inside MAGA
item.description: The war in Iran has added to a tectonic shift in public opinion — a bipartisan swing away from Israel. Some on the far-right are fighting to keep President Trump’s movement aligned with the Jewish state.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Annie Flanagan for The New York Times
item.media:description: Laura Loomer, the far-right media figure, has emerged as one of the president’s most aggressive, pro-Israel enforcers.

item.title: Iran’s Oil Sector and Economy Are Under Pressure as U.S. Blockade Bites
item.description: Tehran says it can endure the pain, but its oil may soon have nowhere to go.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Fatemeh Bahrami/Anadolu Agency, via Getty Images
item.media:description: With the United States blockading Iranian shipping, Iran risks running out of room to store oil in places like Kharg Island.

item.title: Macron Calls for U.S. and Iran to Open Strait of Hormuz While They Negotiate
item.description: Emmanuel Macron, the French president, hopes the Strait of Hormuz can reopen even before the U.S.-Iran war formally ends. His plan has many pitfalls.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Emmanuel Macron of France in Armenia on Tuesday. He has shown frustration about the economic costs of the blocked strait.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands, Erica L. Green and Caroline Houck

item.title: Trump’s U-turn and hopes of a deal ease tensions in energy markets.
item.dc:creator: Qasim Nauman and Erica L. Green
item.media:description: A driver pumped fuel at a gas station in Gresham, Ore., in April.

item.title: Trump and Rubio Insist Iran War Is Over, Even as Missiles Fly During Cease-Fire
item.description: The White House is turning to rhetorical leaps as President Trump tries to put the biggest political crisis of his presidency behind him.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Despite Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s declaration that the objectives of the war have been accomplished, they clearly have not.

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Pausing Efforts to Guide Ships Through Strait Blocked by Iran
item.description: President Trump said a U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz would “remain in full force.” The shift came after the defense secretary said U.S. efforts to free trapped vessels would continue.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green, Eric Schmitt, Greg Jaffe and Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard in Tehran showing the three supreme leaders of Iran last week, only one of whom is alive.

item.title: Iran announces a new system for traffic in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard in Tehran last week, depicting a closed Strait of Hormuz over the mouth of President Trump.

item.title: Trump Looks for a Silver Bullet to End the Iran War. There May Be None.
item.description: The president is trying to ratchet up the economic pressure on Tehran, but Iran’s government is unlikely to make a deal without a big, face-saving compromise.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard in Tehran last week, depicting a closed Strait of Hormuz over the mouth of President Trump.

item.title: The Cheap Guided Rockets U.S. Forces Use Against Iranian Drones
item.description: Called the Advanced Precision Kill Weapon System, it adds laser guidance to a weapon first used in the Korean War.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: An Advanced Precision Kill Weapon System on display at an air show in London in 2024.

item.title: What We Know About ‘Project Freedom’ in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday abruptly paused an American initiative to help guide stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Ships in the Strait of Hormuz near Musandam, Oman, last week. Some 1,600 vessels are believed to be stranded.

item.title: The Growing Rift Between the UAE and Saudi Arabia, Explained
item.description: Officials say competition between the countries is healthy. But tensions are mounting over energy quotas, regional conflicts and their different visions for the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.media:description: A photograph released by Saudi Royal Court showing Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, right, and the Emirati leader, Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed, in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, in 2023.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green, Megan Mineiro, Eric Schmitt and Greg Jaffe

item.title: A Portfolio of Work From The Times’s Pulitzer Prize-Winning Photographer
item.description: The photographer Saher Alghorra reported on the Gaza conflict while enduring the dire conditions his images portrayed.
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Displaced Palestinians began returning to the north of Gaza after the Israeli military announced that a cease-fire was in effect.

item.title: U.S. Shot Down Iranian Missiles and Drones Aimed at American Vessels in Strait of Hormuz, Admiral Says
item.description: An exchange of fire threatened to shatter a fragile cease-fire as President Trump seeks to break Iran’s effective blockade of the waterway.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Ships and tankers in the Strait of Hormuz in April.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Ismaeel Naar and Eric Schmitt

item.title: U.S. Warns China Over Iranian Oil as Sanctions Fight Intensifies
item.description: China told its independent refineries to disregard U.S. sanctions over their purchases of Iranian crude.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The warning came from Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, who is expected to participate in high-stakes meetings this month between the United States and China.

item.title: Strikes in U.A.E., Oman and at Sea Strain Iran Truce to the Breaking Point
item.description: The drone and missile attacks were the first of their kind since the U.S.-Iranian cease-fire began last month. They coincided with reported clashes between American and Iranian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Ismaeel Naar and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Ships have been stranded in the Strait of Hormuz for weeks after Iran effectively blockaded the waterway at the start of the war with the United States and Israel.

item.title: With World Distracted by Iran War, Israeli Settlers Intensify Attacks in West Bank
item.description: Israel has failed to arrest a wave of violence against Palestinians, prompting military officials to urge the government to intervene.
item.dc:creator: Natan Odenheimer, David M. Halbfinger and Fatima AbdulKarim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mourners carrying the body of Amir Odeh, 28, who was killed in an attack by Israeli settlers in the village of Qusra, in the West Bank, in March.

item.title: Pakistan Says It Helped Iranian Crew From Ship That U.S. Seized
item.description: Describing the transfer of the crew from the MV Touska cargo ship back to Iran as a ‘confidence-building measure’ between U.S. and Iranian officials, Pakistan again plays a mediating role.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier, Dan Watson and Zia ur-Rehman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A photo released by the U.S. Central Command showing the Iranian-flagged MV Touska container ship.

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Will Help Stranded Ships Leave Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump offered few details on how the new program, which he called Project Freedom, would work, but said interference would be dealt with “forcefully.”
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, David E. Sanger and Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, near Musandam, Oman, on Friday.

item.title: China Seeks an Advantage With Both Trump and Iran as War Evolves
item.description: With President Trump’s visit to Beijing looming, China is pushing Iran to negotiate even as its companies export material that could be used by Iran’s military.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A government-organized march in Tehran on Wednesday in support of Iran’s new supreme leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei.
